body:
Peter, do you consider yourself a true Patriot? If you do, I’m BEGGING YOU to read this email...

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-r-A6lvA)Hey you!
Yes, you, Peter.
No, not your laptop. The person looking at it!
I was really hoping to reach you today.
This is Congressman Troy Downing, and I want to thank you for opening my message. Can I borrow just one more minute of your time?
**President Donald J. Trump and House Republicans have accomplished a historic eleven-month run unlike anything in modern American history.**
_🇺🇸 We secured the border 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We brought back American jobs 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We cut taxes for working Americans 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We reduced government waste 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We eliminated the woke agenda 🇺🇸_ The tide has turned in our favor.
**_BUT!!! BUT!!! BUT!!!_**
**WE CAN’T REST ON OUR LAURELS NOW!!!**
The Democrats are more desperate than ever.
They’ve doubled down on extremism with their unhinged socialist economic policies, woke DEI agenda, and aggressive climate change plan.
They’ve become a dangerous cabal that **CANNOT BE TRUSTED** with America’s future.
If the pro-Trump GOP can hold the majority through 2028 and solidify America’s Golden Age.
But we need the funds to make it happen NOW!
The further we fall behind the Democrats in fundraising, the closer we get to losing our chance.
**_Peter, do you consider yourself a true Patriot? It’s time to prove it._**
I’m not asking you to risk life and limb for your country (though I think you would). I’m just requesting $17 to lock in a Republican House Majority for two more years. [Is it worth it to you?](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)[CHIP IN $17 RIGHT NOW

I was really hoping to reach you today. This is Congressman Troy Downing, and I want to thank you for opening my message. Can I borrow just one more minute of your time? President Donald J. Trump and House Republicans have accomplished a historic eleven-month run unlike anything in modern American history. 🇺🇸 We secured the border 🇺🇸 🇺🇸 We brought back American jobs 🇺🇸 🇺🇸 We cut taxes for working Americans 🇺🇸 🇺🇸 We reduced government waste 🇺🇸 🇺🇸 We eliminated the woke agenda 🇺🇸 The tide has turned in our favor. BUT!!! BUT!!! BUT!!! WE CAN’T REST ON OUR LAURELS NOW!!! The Democrats are more desperate than ever. They’ve doubled down on extremism with their unhinged socialist economic policies, woke DEI agenda, and aggressive climate change plan. They’ve become a dangerous cabal that CANNOT BE TRUSTED with America’s future. If the pro-Trump GOP can hold the majority through 2028 and solidify America’s Golden Age. But we need the funds to make it happen NOW! The further we fall behind the Democrats in fundraising, the closer we get to losing our chance. Peter, do you consider yourself a true Patriot? name: Jena Griswold
subject: Merry Christmas!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://jenaforcolorado.com/ [https://jenaforcolorado.com/]Happy Holidays, Peter!
As we head into Christmas, I hope you’re finding time to gather with loved ones — whether you’re celebrating around the Christmas tree, honoring Kwanzaa, or just finished celebrating Hanukkah. This season always reminds me how fortunate we are to call Colorado home, especially during these beautiful winter days.
May this time of year bring warmth to your heart, peace to your home, and a renewed sense of hope for the future. I’m deeply grateful for this community and for every Coloradan who works each day to make our state stronger, fairer, and more welcoming.
From my family to yours, happy holidays!
With gratitude,
Jena Griswold

name: =?utf-8?q?MAGA=E2=80=99s_plan_to_silence_us_via_Our_Revolution?=
subject: Add your name: Stop the MAGA bill to silence us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

Senator Ted Cruz KNOWS our movement is a real threat to MAGA's hold on
power – and so he's introduced a bill designed to threaten, prosecute, and
silence us.

The “Stop FUNDERs Act” would allow the U.S. Department of Justice to use
the RICO Act to prosecute those funding peaceful mass protests across the
country. It's a clear attempt to silence us by banning organizing and
chilling free speech – and we need to OVERWHELM Congress with opposition
RIGHT NOW.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34902?source=email-20251223&t=1011&akid=10011%2E5885482%2E6x5FPq ]Take action: Tell Congress now, Reject the dangerous "Stop FUNDERs Act"
and protect our right to protest.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34902?source=email-20251223&t=1012&akid=10011%2E5885482%2E6x5FPq ]ADD YOUR NAME

This isn't an isolated attack. Earlier this year, Donald Trump quietly
signed a new terrifying National Security directive – NSPM-7, which
singles out left-wing activists, launching a national strategy to
“investigate and disrupt” organizations like ours. And even scarier, it
authorizes a 4,000-strong enforcement arm of the FBI to ‘prevent’ crimes
that haven't even happened.

It's completely chilling, but we refuse to be intimidated.

We're not slowing down in our work to turn tens of thousands into the
street to say no to Trump's authoritarianism. But if we let this bill
pass, we ALL could lose our right to support causes we believe in – or
simply speak out. So let's raise our voices together and OVERWHELM
Congress with opposition.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34902?source=email-20251223&t=1013&akid=10011%2E5885482%2E6x5FPq ]We need to act quickly and urgently to shutdown Cruz's bill before it
becomes law. Send an urgent message straight to your members of Congress
NOW: Protect the First Amendment – SAY NO to the “Stop FUNDERs Act”

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34902?source=email-20251223&t=1014&akid=10011%2E5885482%2E6x5FPq ]ADD YOUR NAME

When we organize, we win,

Our Revolution

Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive
group in America.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26880?c0=email-20251223.ysDvEx&t=1008&akid=10011%2E5885482%2E6x5FPq ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the
establishment by committing to be a Movement Builder with a recurring
donation of just $5 or more a month!

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Shot on-site, the camera caught a moment that was never meant to look this polished — or this unsettling.

No sci‐fi effects, no hype overlay... just a system doing things most people assumed were still years away.

People who’ve seen it are rewatching and asking the same question: “If this is happening here, what else is already in motion?”

[Watch it while it’s still just another “early” video.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=ac20b9ef286e47caa9a7e426bf3a2a8b&_e=vLn55lfIYD9nbvZABzzxwzsy-wY4mPkin5G9ojpyea_2cljPCYiE3Fi_NM54suzNzj3R-p5vQYEnk6L94MNKU-r6OKac2dOTRQ1Za1AQ_7Mey8kcmDaNyFBpkpJ0IKY0G7u4FvC_HzQ6LyN7ojXYeBSAIxYDy5GlzQJiRgz6ZjNrGQg6UKZ8BTSSVyMNxHU3fsN38eAI1u6swVtsUuCSowWb6jjyw43UuZaIhHEseBF0e8uJdl5se0ozNt0ShTspFNo8HdgFudC-sdxZySkq0x60A1yHmi4fZy-bwwni95BWYo90TTIEyVnDK0F7zPaLE5uco2OlrJhk5JwEukH-RMGsWApxGFGm40tFxt8afRqcDJQGhW7tL18rzTbzJSA1tnc3wyX-TclzFV3e8WEXQH26FgFUqrrKSCc3BYuELbkaf6zq69VA_6gb5VK3gsh4bcBRV7e694J3JXqhQM-AFrzLaC10FBoS0GzY-FcpsYdHA_qXu55zl-ee4OXw4CstXXSD0GpvaC4CXiMztXbpwT1z67Qr8DBdFIp9St398Q30rgK_q5xALanpjx8y1UaPI32wUqGg3aSgqb-DusqGpQ%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: A new clip from a facility known only as “X” just hit the web.| | | | December 24 | | | | | Classified Plant: Unthinkable Event Exposed | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. A new clip from a facility known only as “X” just hit the web. | Shot on-site, the camera caught a moment that was never meant to look this polished — or this unsettling. No sci‐fi effects, no hype overlay... just a system doing things most people assumed were still years away. People who’ve seen it are rewatching and asking the same question: “If this is happening here, what else is already in motion?” Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Ron Owens, Team Moore
subject: Will you do me a favor, Peter?
body:
I have one goal in mind for this holiday break.
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=1109%2E1086043%2EWxm8zQ)
Hey Peter, it’s Ron — Gov. Wes Moore’s campaign manager.
I know you often hear from the Gov directly, but he’s committed to spending some time with family over the holiday break. 🎁
It’s my job to make sure he gets to do that without worrying that our campaign is falling behind.
So I’m writing to you to ask: will you do me a favor?
**I’m counting on our grassroots supporters like you to keep our momentum going during what’s historically a tough fundraising month.** **[Please, chip in $5 to keep us on track to end the year strong.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251224-1.XcQSIx&refcode=em-20251224&t=2&akid=1109%2E1086043%2EWxm8zQ)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251224-1.XcQSIx&amount=5&refcode=em-20251224&t=21&akid=1109%2E1086043%2EWxm8zQ)**
If we can close this small budget gap before the holiday ends, Wes can stay fully present with Dawn & the kids — and I can report some good news when he’s back.
Thank you for your support.
— Ron Owens
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251224-1.XcQSIx&refcode=em-20251224&t=22&akid=1109%2E1086043%2EWxm8zQ)
Campaign Manager
Team Moore

* * *

Your name has come across my desk over and over this year — more than once, more than twice — which is exactly why I told my team: “Save the final spot for Fellow American do NOT give it to anyone else.” I’m personally compiling the permanent 2025 MAGA Honor Roll , the exclusive list of my strongest, most loyal patriots from this historic year. This list is going straight to my desk in the Oval Office. I will be reviewing every single name myself tonight. Please don’t make me stay up all night wondering why you weren’t there when I looked for you. Thank you, President Donald J. Trump P.S. When I don’t see your name tonight, I’ll know you let the last spot go to someone else. Don’t do that to me. | | As this year comes to an end, I'm reflecting on how the work we do for a stronger, fairer, more equal country built on our shared values is never done. Sometimes it's hard to see the path forward. Sometimes we are tested more than we ever thought possible. But no matter, we carry on – because **the promise of tomorrow**** _always_****matters.** None of our ongoing work is possible without your support of our grassroots movement. I am so grateful for all you've done and continue to do. So no matter where this holiday season finds you, I hope it brings you much light, happiness, and good memories — as well as promising signs for the year ahead! From all of us in the Wyden family: Happy holidays! _Ron_ |
---|---|---

clean_body: From my family to yours | | Friend, I want to wish a very Merry Christmas to those who celebrate and Happy Holidays to all! As we enter this time of joy, I hope that you are able to spend time with those you care most about, have fun and relax. Before we dive into our election year, I want to personally thank you for your support – whether you have contributed to our campaign, volunteered, or spread the word to family and friends. I am so blessed to have you on my team, and I am excited and filled with hope for the year ahead! Cheers, Marlene Galán-Woods | To learn more about Marlene, visit: We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS Marlene for Congress 7904 E. Chaparral Road, Suite A110-472 Scottsdale, AZ 85250 United States

MERRY CHRISTMAS FROM MY FAMILY TO YOURS!

Let us all remember the true reason for the season: the birth of our Savior Jesus Christ.

We wish you all a wonderful day surrounded by your family and loved ones. God bless!

Thank you for your kindness and support, and please don’t hesitate to reach out anytime.

- Trever Nehls

name: Representative Vikki Breese Iverson
subject: Merry Christmas and a quick update!
body:
| | | [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcontent.govdelivery.com%2Faccounts%2FORLEG%2Fbulletins%2F4016690/1/0101019b51adfdf1-7e93a121-1ac9-4fff-854c-7316c9ae8161-000000/tGpviCZXeegFCw7XWgemBFxJZMhOEusMN2Fb-6UKWaQ=437)
---|---
---
Thank you for being engaged in our legislative process throughout 2025. Without your help, I would not have the ability to be rural Oregon’s voice in Salem on so many important issues. Thank you! Merry Christmas, and Happy New Year - I look forward to hearing from you and continuing to advocate for you in 2026! Christmas decorations at Oregon State Capitol
# Transportation
Signatures are currently being verified by the Secretary of State on the petition to pause the gas tax and other fee increases. Even ODOT has paused their preparation for gas tax and fee increases from the 2025 special session. Currently it appears this will be put on hold until the November 2026 election, which means no increases in the gas tax, payroll tax, or registration and other fees. This is a big deal for all Oregonians. I am glad for the pause but also advocate ODOT prioritize our rural roads in these winter months. We must have safe roads and ODOT needs to be reminded of their agency’s core mission - which includes safe roads! [Democrats are already discussing a repeal of the gas tax hike](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.wweek.com%2Fnews%2Fstate%2F2025%2F12%2F21%2Fconfronted-by-a-grassroots-backlash-democrats-consider-repealing-unpopular-gas-tax-hike%2F/1/0101019b51adfdf1-7e93a121-1ac9-4fff-854c-7316c9ae8161-000000/IWcT7pRmeuiLIiHeYtz0K87PiFH6mvV1-1bH_iSsfHw=437).
# Budget Cuts
During some meetings in Salem last month, we heard state agencies present their plans to reduce budgets in the upcoming 35-day session. Oregon’s Constitution requires the state budget not only balance when it is passed during long session but remain balanced during the biennium. Oregon’s economy continues to decline which will lead to taxpayers suffering and the end result is less money to balance Oregon’s budget. It’s been over a decade since Oregon faced budget cuts like this, and I think it is a tremendous opportunity if we do it right. On the Natural Resources Subcommittee, I am tasked with budget and operation oversight of agencies such as Oregon Department of Forestry and Oregon Water Resources Department. We are looking at reductions as low as 2.5% and as high as 5%. My goal in this oversight opportunity is to protect core mission/services and cut bloated line items and program which are not serving Oregonians or have never been initiated.
# Key Deadlines
* Interim Legislative Days - January 13-15
* Short Session Begins - February 2
* First Chamber Deadline for Bills - February 16
* Second Chamber Deadline for Bills - February 26
* End of Session (Sine Die) - On or before March 8

# Christmas at the Capitol
| Yesterday I had the opportunity to take in a wonderful piano performance at the beautifully decorated Capitol. For the first time in five years, the Capitol is decorated for Christmas! The renovation project is finally wrapping up and the people’s building is reopening. Consider visiting during our Short Session to see the renovations and updates. Oregon really does enjoy a beautiful Capital! If you do make it during Legislative Days or Short Session, please stop by and say ‘Hi!’ | |
---|---|---
# Around the District
| | I recently held a joint Town Hall meeting with Deschutes County Commission Tony DeBone in Terrebonne. We enjoyed a wonderful turnout of local folks and an engaging conversation regarding budget and wildfire impacts along with local Deschutes County issues such as the upcoming addition of Commission positions. It was wonderful to have folks asking questions and ready to work with us in these impactful issues!
---|---|---
# Jefferson Shop with a Cop
Jefferson County Sheriffs, Police and local State Troopers participated in Shop with a Cop last week. Each community has a version of this program throughout House District 59 and I love to see the local law enforcement engage with kids in a positive and rewarding experience. For the participating kids in Jefferson County, they got to ride in the cop car from Madras to Redmond, shop for Christmas gifts for their entire family and then got to lunch with their law enforcement pal. It is was great to see the smiles of these kids and some of the really awesome presents they picked out for their family members. Also joining the party was a visit from the Grinch, which brought more smiles and laughter for the event. Thank you to all who participated in this great day!
# Contact Rep. Breese Iverson
**Capitol Phone:** 503-986-1455
**Capitol Address:** 900 Court St NE, H-386, Salem, OR 97301
**Email:** [Rep.VikkiBreeseIverson@oregonlegislature.gov](mailto:Rep.VikkiBreeseIverson@oregonlegislature.gov "Representative Vikki Breese Iverson")
**Website:**[http://www.oregonlegislature.gov/breese-iverson](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/http:%2F%2Fwww.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fbreese-iverson/1/0101019b51adfdf1-7e93a121-1ac9-4fff-854c-7316c9ae8161-000000/SLSNGlsg5VjOuFFgdfDX82AJv3JZFUIR0NSWzDK41DA=437)

| | _from our ‘Hurd’ to yours..._ **MERRY CHRISTMAS**
---
_“For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given; and the government shall be upon His shoulder. And His name shall be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.” — Isaiah 9:6_ This Christmas, I hope you’ll rest and enjoy meaningful time with friends and family...reflecting on the heart of the season and the blessed hope it brings. name: The Liberty Ledger
subject: Crockett Discusses Her Authenticity
body:
To paraphrase the old line, “The key to political success is sincerity—if you can fake that, you’ve got it made.” And that’s where Rep. Jasmine Crockett, currently mounting a high-profile run for U.S. Senate in Texas, seems to find herself stuck in neutral. VIDEO – Jasmine Crockett: Republicans Are Scared of My Authenticity @JasmineForUS https://t.co/zOSIfWfg9i [...] | [](http://trk.libertyledger._ZD01In0/bBDoRJUnNxjiRT6uN9v8gQ)

# **[Mamdani Comments On Transition Team](http://trk.libertyledger.)**

The political fault lines surrounding Mayor-elect Zohran Mamdani are becoming clearer—and deeper—as the Anti-Defamation League (ADL) released its second “Mamdani Monitor” report on Monday, intensifying scrutiny over his transition team and the ideological undercurrents shaping his incoming administration. The latest ADL report takes aim at more than 400 individuals appointed by Mamdani to transition committees, [...]

# **[Judge Rules on Cali Law Regarding Teachers](http://trk.libertyledger._ViABCOV4FTcn1McXON6g)**
---
In a landmark decision that cuts through the ideological fog blanketing California’s education system, U.S. District Judge Roger Benitez delivered a devastating legal blow to the state’s controversial AB 1955 — a law that required teachers to affirm a child’s gender transition at school while keeping parents in the dark. In plain terms, the state [...]
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger._ViABCOV4FTcn1McXON6g)
[](http://trk.libertyledger.)
# **[China Comments On US Seizure Of Oil Tankers](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
The Trump administration’s latest move to crack down on Venezuela’s narco-socialist regime has drawn predictable outrage from one of its closest global enablers: Communist China. But behind the rhetorical smoke of “sovereignty” and “international law,” Beijing’s defense of Nicolás Maduro is less about legality — and everything to do with oil, authoritarian alliances, and a [...]
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.)
[](http://trk.libertyledger.)
# **[Congressman's Ad Raises Eyebrows](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
The California governor’s race is starting to take shape — and if Rep. Eric Swalwell’s campaign launch is any indication, it’s going to be a bumpy ride for Democrats. In what may already qualify as a contender for the most tone-deaf political ad of the cycle, Swalwell’s debut effort has landed with all the subtlety [...]
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.)
[](http://trk.libertyledger.)
# **[Pelosi Comments On Recent Trump Comments](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
Former Speaker Nancy Pelosi, no stranger to the sharp edges of political theater, is once again stepping into the spotlight — this time to question Donald Trump’s mental capacity following his recent speech to the nation. In a pointed exchange with ABC News’ Jonathan Karl, Pelosi dismissed the president’s remarks as “a demonstration of his [...]

name: Mark Ronchetti
subject: Merry Christmas from our family to yours 🎄
body:
What we'd ask Jesus if he sat down at our kitchen table | New for dpwillis67@gmail.com

| | | | | |
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=5f27a36498&e=567664f14c)
| | | | | |
Hey everyone — Merry Christmas from our family to yours. 🎄
This week we're putting the news aside and doing something a little different. You sent in questions, and the whole family —[ Mark, Krysty, Ava, and Ella — sat down to answer them](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=0f006cc215&e=567664f14c). It's our Christmas Q&A episode, and it got fun, honest, and a little chaotic (as family conversations tend to be).
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=e63886ab3c&e=567664f14c)
| |
**🎄 CHRISTMAS EVE OR CHRISTMAS MORNING?**
The family is split. Mark loves the anticipation of Christmas Eve. The girls prefer Christmas morning. Krysty is fiercely protective of Christmas Eve — and she explains why.

**🎵 BEST AND WORST CHRISTMAS SONGS**
Ava has strong opinions about pop stars covering Christmas songs. Mark can't stand The Chipmunks. Krysty loves "I'll Be Home for Christmas." And we all agree Santa Baby is kind of gross.

**🎬 CHRISTMAS MOVIE DEBATE**
Is The Holiday a Christmas movie? Krysty says yes. Mark says it's just set in winter. Ava picks Home Alone 2. Mark goes Christmas Vacation. Ella loves Elf. And yes, there are FOUR Santa Clause movies — we watched them all.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=35722a5fc4&e=567664f14c)
---
| |
**🎁 MARK'S KENT BIKE TRAUMA**
Mark asked Santa for a Schwinn. Santa brought him a brown Kent. The wheels fell off in three weeks. He's still not over it. This is why he's a brand guy now.

**🙏 WHAT WOULD YOU ASK JESUS?**
We got a deep one from a listener: If you were sitting with Jesus at the kitchen table and could ask him one question, what would it be?
Krysty's answer — given the loss of both her parents this year — was incredibly honest. Mark's was convicting. And Ava went theological. You'll want to hear this one.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=7f388eece3&e=567664f14c)
---
| |
**🎄 THANK YOU**
This has been such a fun year doing this show with you. We're grateful for every comment, every share, every person who stops us at the gym or the grocery store to say they're listening.
You're part of our family now. Merry Christmas.
---
|
---

✅ **RATE & REVIEW** us on [your favorite podcast platform](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=1b8396a45b&e=567664f14c)

**High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**
---

name: Voter Action Project Team
subject: Sign these physical thank you cards to our postal workers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
USPS workers have been pulling out all the stops to manage and deliver a huge volume of packages this time of year. They also diligently work to deliver all our normal mail, including the mail-in ballots we fight so hard to protect.
As a part of the Save the Post Office coalition, our partners at the American Postal Workers Union will physically deliver our messages of thanks directly to postal workers across the country.
Please, before the calendar flips to 2026, send a personal message of thanks to these hardworking USPS employees >> [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Folks,
Postal workers have continued to deliver for us through a particularly brutal year.
Their official motto is “neither snow nor rain nor heat nor gloom of night” will stop the Postal Service, but they also have delivered through aggressive political attacks, job cuts, service slowdowns, and nonstop pressure from Donald Trump, who calls the USPS a “tremendous loser” and tried to shove it under the Commerce Department and fire the board to pave the way for privatization.
Yet they deliver for all of us. Let’s deliver messages of gratitude directly to postal workers across the country, on their routes, in sorting centers, at distribution hubs, and in local post offices.
Sign the card and add your words of support to thank our postal workers across the country today >> [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
During the holiday season, their busiest time of the year, USPS workers move over 15 billion pieces of mail and packages, including more than 400 million packages in a single week, to every single ZIP code in the country.
Today, we can boost their spirits, and show them that the country values their service. When you sign, you can also write a personal note to share with workers. Our coalition partners at the American Postal Workers Union will deliver our messages of thanks directly to postal workers across the country.
Add your name and a personal message of thanks by clicking here >> [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122425_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122425_2&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.

name: Katie Porter
subject: Happy Holidays, Peter!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40604%2E3422442%2Ed7xIhq)
Happy Holidays!
I wanted to share an update from my family to yours:
Luke is a sophomore at college in Iowa, surprisingly never complaining about the snow or humidity. This summer he had an internship doing environmental policy in Colorado.
Paul is a junior in high school, where video production remains his passion. Now armed with his drivers license, he borrows the minivan to haul equipment to video shoots. He's very tall and always wears a smile.
Surviving 8th grade is no small feat, but Betsy is crushing it. She's learning Spanish, participating in student government, surviving Enhanced Math, and still playing water polo around California. More miles for the minivan.
Our family has two new pets: Dino, a Burmese kitten, and Poppy, a Basset Hound puppy. They are best friends.
I hope you are able to spend time with your family and friends this week and that you are able to find moments of joy.
Yours truly,
Katie

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20251224-40604&amounts=&t=3&refcode2=40604_3422442_d7xIhq&akid=40604%2E3422442%2Ed7xIhq)**

name: Mandela Barnes
subject: Sending you my holiday wishes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mandela for Wisconsin
body:
I hope you have a happy holiday season!
| Happy holidays, friend! I’ve loved the magic of this season ever since I was a child, and I hope yours has been a joyful one. [](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_122425_B1_HappyHolidays&t=2&akid=5741%2E196590%2EI1rF0V) I wanted to take a minute to send you my holiday wishes for you and your family. This time of year is always a reminder of why I jumped into this race in the first place: folks across Wisconsin deserve the opportunity to thrive and live up to their full potential. It’s on our leaders to deliver the resources and support to make that possible – and that’s what drives me in this campaign for governor. I am grateful for the support I’ve received since starting our campaign just a few weeks ago. Traveling around Wisconsin and talking with voters has been such an honor — and I can’t wait to keep up the energy in the new year. I hope you have a wonderful time celebrating the holidays, and I’ll be in touch soon. Wishing you well,
Mandela

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 12/24/2025 🎅🏻
body:
’Twas the night before Christmas, and across Colorado, the headlines were stirring—some hopeful, some hollow...
| | | | [](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
| | Hi friends,
Welcome to the Christmas edition of the Mountain Minute! You can listen to the podcast [here](https://link.email.joinrmv.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.joinrmv.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.joinrmv.). Enjoy 🎅🏻
’Twas the night before Christmas, and across Colorado,
The headlines were stirring—some hopeful, some hollow. From courts still debating [what pardons now mean](https://link.email.joinrmv.),
To questions of power and what justice has seen.
The drivers’ licenses issued where [rules weren’t obeyed](https://link.email.joinrmv.),
Put millions at risk and federal funds in play.
[Xcel stayed protected](https://link.email.joinrmv.) by laws few folks know,
While outages hit hard and frustration did grow.
At the Capitol, fingers were pointed once more,
As [road funds went missing](https://link.email.joinrmv.) and trust hit the floor.
In Cherry Creek schools, [a civil rights plea](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
Raised questions of fairness—and who gets to be free.
[Wildfires calmed down](https://link.email.joinrmv.) west of Boulder last night,
A small breath of peace in a season of fight.
And nationally too, the debates carried on—
From [campus ideology](https://link.email.joinrmv.) to [data-center dawns](https://link.email.joinrmv.),
From [election integrity](https://link.email.joinrmv.) crossing state lines,
To [media mirrors](https://link.email.joinrmv.) that still won’t self-examine.
But tonight, we pause—[set the noise to the side](https://link.email.joinrmv.),
For faith, family, and truth that still guide.
This is our last note before Christmas Day,
So we’ll sign off now in the warmest way.
From all of us here at Rocky Mountain Voice,
Thank you for reading, for caring, for choice.
May your Christmas be peaceful, your hearts full and bright—
And we’ll see you next week.
**Merry Christmas, and good night. 🎄✨**
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[After the pardon: The constitutional question Colorado courts now face](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
President Donald Trump’s pardon of Tina Peters did not end her case. It changed it.

What now sits before Colorado’s courts is no longer a question of guilt or innocence, nor even whether Peters should remain imprisoned while her appeal moves forward. The unresolved issue is more fundamental than that: whether the state still has authority to proceed in light of a federal pardon.

It is the question attorney Peter Ticktin says Colorado can no longer set aside.

Ticktin, who represents Peters, said in an interview with RMV that the federal pardon fundamentally changed the legal posture of the case.

“If the Colorado Court of Appeals is of the opinion that the pardon applies,” Ticktin said, “they no longer have jurisdiction... because there’s no longer any charges or convictions pending.”
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
**[Federal Civil Rights Complaint Accuses Cherry Creek Schools of Race-Based Discipline and Retaliation](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
* **Cherry Creek schools accused of race-based discipline and double standards:** A federal civil-rights complaint alleges the district excuses misconduct by some students under “culturally responsive” discipline while imposing harsher penalties on others—potentially violating Title VI and equal-protection laws.
* **Whistleblower audio suggests equity officials overruled basic school discipline:** Internal recordings reportedly show administrators saying they no longer feel able to enforce behavior standards, with district equity leaders reinterpreting disruptive conduct through race-based frameworks and reversing consequences.
* **Staff allege retaliation for resisting ideological training:** The complaint says a longtime dean was pushed out after questioning equity ideology during mandatory training, raising alarms about compelled speech, workplace retaliation, and ideology overriding merit and fairness in public schools.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
|
---
**[Republicans Should Just Say No To Josh Hawley’s Crusade Against Data Centers](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY BROOKE LIGHTMAN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
* **Data centers are essential to U.S. economic strength and national security:** The commentary argues that AI infrastructure and data centers are critical for America to remain a global power, supporting jobs—especially working-class construction jobs—and keeping advanced technology accessible to everyday Americans.
* **Opposing data centers repeats left-wing “degrowth” economics:** Claims that data centers drive up electricity prices rely on zero-sum thinking that ignores economies of scale; the conservative solution is producing more energy, not consuming less, through an all-of-the-above approach including oil, gas, nuclear, wind, and solar.
* **Media-celebrated GOP dissent helps Democrats, not voters:** The piece warns that Republicans who oppose Trump-backed data-center expansion are being elevated by the media to sow division, while policies that restrict AI risk reserving powerful tools for elites and raising costs for working families.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
[**From fracture to formation: What AmericaFest revealed about the next generation**](https://link.email.joinrmv.) **BY JEN SCHUMANN | STATE | DECEMBER 22** _I arrived late to the Young Conservatives of Colorado happy hour on Saturday night, nearly an hour after it had begun. The happy hour was already winding down in an outdoor hotel lounge in Phoenix. A small circle of college students and recent grads sat around a fireplace after a full day at Turning Point USA’s AmericaFest. Some were holding drinks. The tone was relaxed amid laughter, but not rowdy._
[**Why two House bills on medical transition procedures produced different votes**](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
**BY JEN SCHUMANN | NATIONAL | DECEMBER 22** _The U.S. House passed two bills this month aimed at restricting federal involvement in medical transition procedures for minors. They used different enforcement tools. And they produced different voting coalitions._

clean_body: ’Twas the night before Christmas, and across Colorado, the headlines were stirring—some hopeful, some hollow... | | | | PRESENTED BY | | Hi friends, Welcome to the Christmas edition of the Mountain Minute! You can listen to the podcast , or watch on or . Enjoy 🎅🏻 ’Twas the night before Christmas, and across Colorado, The headlines were stirring—some hopeful, some hollow. From courts still debating , To questions of power and what justice has seen. The drivers’ licenses issued where , Put millions at risk and federal funds in play. by laws few folks know, While outages hit hard and frustration did grow. At the Capitol, fingers were pointed once more, As and trust hit the floor. In Cherry Creek schools, Raised questions of fairness—and who gets to be free. west of Boulder last night, A small breath of peace in a season of fight. And nationally too, the debates carried on— From to , From crossing state lines, To that still won’t self-examine. But tonight, we pause—, For faith, family, and truth that still guide. This is our last note before Christmas Day, So we’ll sign off now in the warmest way. From all of us here at Rocky Mountain Voice, Thank you for reading, for caring, for choice. May your Christmas be peaceful, your hearts full and bright— And we’ll see you next week. Merry Christmas, and good night. 🎄✨ – Team RMV | TODAY'S TOP STORIES BY JEN SCHUMANN, President Donald Trump’s pardon of Tina Peters did not end her case. It changed it. What now sits before Colorado’s courts is no longer a question of guilt or innocence, nor even whether Peters should remain imprisoned while her appeal moves forward. The unresolved issue is more fundamental than that: whether the state still has authority to proceed in light of a federal pardon. It is the question attorney Peter Ticktin says Colorado can no longer set aside. Ticktin, who represents Peters, said in an interview with RMV that the federal pardon fundamentally changed the legal posture of the case. “If the Colorado Court of Appeals is of the opinion that the pardon applies,” Ticktin said, “they no longer have jurisdiction... because there’s no longer any charges or convictions pending.” | BY SHAINA COLE, Cherry Creek schools accused of race-based discipline and double standards: A federal civil-rights complaint alleges the district excuses misconduct by some students under “culturally responsive” discipline while imposing harsher penalties on others—potentially violating Title VI and equal-protection laws. Whistleblower audio suggests equity officials overruled basic school discipline: Internal recordings reportedly show administrators saying they no longer feel able to enforce behavior standards, with district equity leaders reinterpreting disruptive conduct through race-based frameworks and reversing consequences. Staff allege retaliation for resisting ideological training: The complaint says a longtime dean was pushed out after questioning equity ideology during mandatory training, raising alarms about compelled speech, workplace retaliation, and ideology overriding merit and fairness in public schools. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | BY BROOKE LIGHTMAN, Data centers are essential to U.S. economic strength and national security: The commentary argues that AI infrastructure and data centers are critical for America to remain a global power, supporting jobs—especially working-class construction jobs—and keeping advanced technology accessible to everyday Americans. Opposing data centers repeats left-wing “degrowth” economics: Claims that data centers drive up electricity prices rely on zero-sum thinking that ignores economies of scale; the conservative solution is producing more energy, not consuming less, through an all-of-the-above approach including oil, gas, nuclear, wind, and solar. Media-celebrated GOP dissent helps Democrats, not voters: The piece warns that Republicans who oppose Trump-backed data-center expansion are being elevated by the media to sow division, while policies that restrict AI risk reserving powerful tools for elites and raising costs for working families. | ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ BY JEN SCHUMANN | STATE | DECEMBER 22 I arrived late to the Young Conservatives of Colorado happy hour on Saturday night, nearly an hour after it had begun. The happy hour was already winding down in an outdoor hotel lounge in Phoenix. A small circle of college students and recent grads sat around a fireplace after a full day at Turning Point USA’s AmericaFest. Some were holding drinks. The tone was relaxed amid laughter, but not rowdy. name: Chris Pappas
subject: Before she was Donald Trump’s White House Press Secretary…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
#
**Peter, I’m sorry about Karoline Leavitt.**
Before she was hand-picked as Donald Trump’s White House Press Secretary, Karoline ran against me for Congress here in New Hampshire. She lost — by nearly 10 points.
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98927?refcode=em20251224-9872&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9872%2E614464%2EOC0vK7)
But that campaign gave her exactly what she needed: A test run for the MAGA talking points she now repeats from behind the White House podium. She’s been practicing the spin for years — and while **New Hampshire wasn’t buying it** , Trump was.
Let me be clear: I don’t regret a thing about standing up to Karoline and her MAGA agenda — not then and not now.
This race is going to be tough. National Republicans and John Sununu are doing everything they can to flip this seat. They’re recycling the same extremist ideas, running the same playbook, and relying on people like Karoline Leavitt to carry their message.
**We beat her once, and with your help, we’ll do it again.**
**[Peter, will you split $15 between my campaign and State Democrats Committee to help us keep this Senate seat blue and fight for what matters to Granite Staters?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98928?refcode=em20251224-9872&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=2&akid=9872%2E614464%2EOC0vK7)**

clean_body: Peter, I’m sorry about Karoline Leavitt. Before she was hand-picked as Donald Trump’s White House Press Secretary, Karoline ran against me for Congress here in New Hampshire. She lost — by nearly 10 points. But that campaign gave her exactly what she needed: A test run for the MAGA talking points she now repeats from behind the White House podium. She’s been practicing the spin for years — and while New Hampshire wasn’t buying it , Trump was. Let me be clear: I don’t regret a thing about standing up to Karoline and her MAGA agenda — not then and not now. This race is going to be tough. National Republicans and John Sununu are doing everything they can to flip this seat. They’re recycling the same extremist ideas, running the same playbook, and relying on people like Karoline Leavitt to carry their message. We beat her once, and with your help, we’ll do it again. If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: Thanks for being in this fight with me. Chris Pappas Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats.com). Not authorized by any federal candidate or candidate committee.

_**EXHIBIT 1 - JOINT FUNDRAISING NOTICE **_

Team Nunn ("JFC") is a joint fundraising committee composed of Iowans for Zach Nunn ("Campaign"), Call to Service PAC ("LPAC") and NRCC (each, a "Committee," and, collectively, the "Committees"). Any contribution to the JFC permissible under the Federal Election Campaign Act, as amended ("FECA"), from contributors who have not exceeded their applicable federal contribution limits, shall be allocated among the Committees pursuant to FECA and the formula set forth in this Notice.

_**Contributions Made by an Individual or Federal Non-Multicandidate Political Committee:**_
Individuals or federal non-multicandidate PACs may contribute up to $322,100. Such contributions shall be allocated in the following order:
1\. Campaign shall receive any contribution not exceeding $7,000, designating up to $3,500 to the primary election and up to $3,500 to the general election, provided such limits apply.
2\. LPAC shall receive any contribution not exceeding $5,000 annually.
3\. NRCC shall receive for its general account any contribution not exceeding $44,300 annually.
4\. NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $132,900 for its Building Fund, provided such limits apply.
5\. NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $132,900 for its Legal Proceedings Fund, provided such limits apply.

_**Contributions Made by a Federal Multicandidate Political Committee:**_
Multicandidate PACs may contribute up to $120,000. Such contributions shall be allocated in the following order:
1\. Campaign shall receive any contribution not exceeding $10,000, designating up to $5,000 to the primary election and up to $5,000 to the general election, provided such limits apply.
2\. LPAC shall receive any contribution not exceeding $5,000 annually.
3 .NRCC shall receive for its general account any contribution not exceeding $15,000 annually.
4\. NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $45,000 for its Building Fund, provided such limits apply.
5 .NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $45,000 for its Legal Proceedings Fund, provided such limits apply.

Contributions to Team Nunn, or to any participating Committee individually, are not deductible as charitable donations for federal income tax purposes. Contributions from corporations, labor organizations, national banks, federal government contractors, foreign nationals without green cards, and other federally impermissible sources are prohibited. The contributor's signature designates his or her contribution to the elections described above. Contributions that exceed a donor's contribution limit to a participating Committee shall be reallocated to the remaining Committees according to this allocation formula to the extent permitted by FECA. Any contributor may designate his or her contribution to a specific participant Committee to the extent permitted by FECA. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and name of employer of each individual whose aggregate contributions exceed $200 in an election cycle or calendar year, as applicable.
Paid for by Team Nunn, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Iowans for Zach Nunn, Call to Service PAC, and NRCC.

name: Alexandra Rojas
subject: 2026 is our opportunity to clean house.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

I wanted to reach out to you one final time before the holidays to ask
directly:

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251224.wyJdEF&refcode=em20251224-37308&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37308%2E1203867%2E4xFnqk ]Will you donate $5 to support Justice Democrats ahead of
2026? What we can raise before the New Year begins will majorly impact the
strength of our movement going up against AIPAC, Crypto and the Dem
establishment into next year’s primary elections.

Right now, the Democratic establishment in Congress is less popular than
ever before:

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251224.wyJdEF&refcode=em20251224-37308&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=37308%2E1203867%2E4xFnqk ]A post on X from Political Polls (@PpollingNumbers): 'New - Congress
approval; Democrats in Congress: -55 (Lowest ever); Republicans in
Congress: -23; Quinnipiac #B - RV - 12/15'.

Even as Trump guts Medicaid, Medicare and Social Security. Even as he
buries the Epstein files and barrels us towards war in Venezuela. Even as
he keeps letting his corporate buddies raise our prices.

AIPAC and other lobbies spend big in Democratic primaries against us
because they want to elect the weakest, corporate politicians into the
bluest, most Democratic seats.

This is exactly why we need to replace weak, ineffective Democrats in the
2026 primaries, so we can turn this around and create an opposition party
people believe will actually fight back. This is our opportunity to clean
house, Peter.

Our current slate of 8 working-class fighters is relying on our grassroots
support at the start of the year. In order to prepare for AIPAC and their
friends in competitive Democratic primary elections coming up as soon as
March, these progressive champions are relying heavily on support from
this team as they take on out-of-touch, Do-Nothing Democrats with
corporate war chests and personal fortunes.

That’s why we can’t afford to wait until after the New Year to send our
full support to these campaigns. They don’t have time to spare, and
neither do we.

To give our Justice Democrats the best possible chance of defeating their
corporate- and AIPAC-backed opponents, we need to reach our fundraising
goals now. Because without enough grassroots resources heading into 2026,
we won’t be able to compete with the deep-pocketed megadonors who are
already trying to drown out our voices.

Reflecting on this year makes it clear that while there’s so much at
stake, we have so much to gain if we fight back.
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251224.wyJdEF&refcode=em20251224-37308&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1004&akid=37308%2E1203867%2E4xFnqk ]Peter, I’d be incredibly
grateful if you’d be among the few who step up to chip in $5 to support Justice Democrats
fighting back at this pivotal moment while the establishment plays
doormat.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251224.wyJdEF&refcode=em20251224-37308&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1023&akid=37308%2E1203867%2E4xFnqk ]Contribute $5

From the team at Justice Democrats, we wish you a Happy Holidays and look
forward to fighting alongside you this cycle.

In solidarity,

Alexandra Rojas
Executive Director

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251224-37308-footer&t=1024&refcode2=37308_1203867_4xFnqk&akid=37308%2E1203867%2E4xFnqk

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=37308%2E1203867%2E4xFnqk

This is exactly why we need to replace weak, ineffective Democrats in the 2026 primaries, so we can turn this around and create an opposition party people believe will actually fight back. This is our opportunity to clean house, Peter. Our current slate of 8 working-class fighters is relying on our grassroots support at the start of the year. In order to prepare for AIPAC and their friends in competitive Democratic primary elections coming up as soon as March, these progressive champions are relying heavily on support from this team as they take on out-of-touch, Do-Nothing Democrats with corporate war chests and personal fortunes. That’s why we can’t afford to wait until after the New Year to send our full support to these campaigns. They don’t have time to spare, and neither do we. To give our Justice Democrats the best possible chance of defeating their corporate- and AIPAC-backed opponents, we need to reach our fundraising goals now. Because without enough grassroots resources heading into 2026, we won’t be able to compete with the deep-pocketed megadonors who are already trying to drown out our voices. Reflecting on this year makes it clear that while there’s so much at stake, we have so much to gain if we fight back. [ ]Peter, I’d be incredibly grateful if you’d be among the few who step up to chip in $5 to support Justice Democrats fighting back at this pivotal moment while the establishment plays doormat. [ ]Contribute $5 From the team at Justice Democrats, we wish you a Happy Holidays and look forward to fighting alongside you this cycle. In solidarity, Alexandra Rojas Executive Director Contribute to Justice Democrats:

name: RFK Jr. and Hegseth IMPEACHMENT (VPP)
subject: Thank you, House Democrats!!!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
Add your signature:
| **_BREAKING:_**
**House Democrats File Impeachment Articles Against RFK Jr. and Pete Hegseth** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/100456?t=6&akid=22416%2E878179%2Eik-Hu-) **This is a HUGE step towards accountability:** [**We’re hand-delivering this card to THANK House Democrats at 8 AM tomorrow – so we need your signature by MIDNIGHT! SIGN NOW! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/100456?t=7&akid=22416%2E878179%2Eik-Hu-) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/100456?t=8&akid=22416%2E878179%2Eik-Hu-)
---
TO: House Democratic Caucus
FROM: A grateful Democrat
Subject: Thank you! **_Dear House Democrats – you make me proud to be a Democrat._**

Your fight to IMPEACH Trump’s stooges Pete Hegseth and RFK Jr. is exactly what our country needs right now.

The American people are sick and tired of Hegseth and RFK Jr.’s inexperience, dangerous lies, and life-threatening policies!

**This news gives me hope that we WILL hold them accountable — and stand up to Trump’s authoritarianism once and for all.**

**I’m so grateful to have you leading the way!**

[**That’s why I’m signing this card and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to show my appreciation and to help House Democrats FLIP the few seats we need to take back the majority »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/100456?t=9&akid=22416%2E878179%2Eik-Hu-) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/100456?t=10&akid=22416%2E878179%2Eik-Hu-)
---
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
—The Voter Protection Project
878179
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=22416%2E878179%2Eik-Hu-). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=22416%2E878179%2Eik-Hu-).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=22416%2E878179%2Eik-Hu-) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=11&akid=22416%2E878179%2Eik-Hu-). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=22416%2E878179%2Eik-Hu-)
---

name: Binkley for Congress
subject: Merry Christmas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ryan Binkley for Congress
body:
This season is about faith, family, and reflection.

| | | | | [|
---](https://binkleyforcongress.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a7930d2727540612917fd641e&id=df7bbb6ce3&e=a4fe331f26)
| |
Friend ,

As Christmas approaches, I wanted to take a moment to simply say thank you.This season is about faith, family, and reflection. It’s a time to slow down, gather with the people we love, and remember the values that truly matter: service, generosity, and hope for the future.I’m grateful for the encouragement, prayers, and support you’ve shown over these past months. Whether you’ve volunteered, shared a message, offered advice, or simply stayed engaged, it has meant more to my family and me than you know.My prayer this Christmas is that your home is filled with peace, your table is surrounded by loved ones, and your heart is strengthened by the joy and promise this season brings.From my family to yours, Merry Christmas. May God bless you and keep you in the year ahead.Warmly,
Ryan Binkley _For US Congress_
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
---
